flux_1d_ppm.accuracy: O(h^3) on smooth profiles where the CW84 limiter is inactive; degrades to O(h) at active monotonicity-limiter constraints and inside two cells of any boundary that the ghost-fill cannot reach.
flux_1d_ppm.reference: Colella & Woodward (1984), JCP 54(1):174-201, eqs. (1.5)-(1.10): 4th-order interior interpolation of cell-averaged data to the cell edge, sub-grid parabolic profile, and the (1.7)-(1.10) monotonicity limiter. The Courant-fraction flux integral (parabolic profile averaged over the swept volume `c·dx`) is the standard PPM upwind flux of CW84 §4. Flux-form 1D advection composition follows Lin & Rood (1996) MWR. Reference imperative implementation in src/operators/flux_1d.jl (`flux_1d_ppm!`, `flux_1d_ppm_arrayop`, `_build_ppm_face_expr_xi/eta`, `_ppm_limit_cw84_sym`) on a CubedSphereGrid; the Cartesian core encoded here matches that computation in-panel verbatim.
composes: ppm_reconstruction (unlimited 4th-order edge values; same coefficients as discretizations/finite_volume/ppm_reconstruction.json)
colella_woodward_1984 limiter (closed-form ifelse AST; matches discretizations/finite_volume/vertical_remap.json `limiter` block bit-for-bit at the AST level)
Courant-fraction flux integral (CW84 §4) — area-averaged value swept through the interface over one time step
upwind selection by ifelse on sign of `$c`

stencil_support.comment: Six cell-averaged values are consumed at each interface. Indexed relative to the *right cell* of the face (offset 0); the face F_{i+1/2} is the right edge of cell i (offset 0) and the left edge of cell i+1 (offset 1).

selectors.role: qm3 — left tail for the left-cell q_left edge value
qm2 — extreme left of the qi_half stencil
qm1 — left cell of the interface (cell i)
q0 — right cell of the interface (cell i+1)
qp1 — extreme right of the qi_half stencil
qp2 — right tail for the right-cell q_right edge value

edge_value_stencil.comment: CW84 eq. (1.6): 4th-order interpolation of cell-averaged data to the interface F_{i+1/2}. Same coefficients as ppm_reconstruction.json `stencil.q_right_edge`. The interior-cell q_left / q_right edges are obtained by shifting this stencil one cell.
edge_value_stencil.produces: qi_half  (interface value between qm1 and q0)

left_cell_edges.comment: Edges of the *left* cell (cell i, offset -1). The right edge of cell i is qi_half; the left edge uses the stencil shifted one cell to the left.

ql_L_raw.comment: Left edge of cell i — same 4-point CW84 form as qi_half, shifted by -1.

right_cell_edges.comment: Edges of the *right* cell (cell i+1, offset 0). The left edge of cell i+1 is qi_half; the right edge uses the stencil shifted one cell to the right.

qr_R_raw.comment: Right edge of cell i+1 — same 4-point CW84 form as qi_half, shifted by +1.

limiter.comment: Colella & Woodward (1984) §4 monotonicity limiter applied independently to each upwind cell's reconstruction. Closed-form ExpressionNode AST so the rule is evaluator-agnostic; matches src/operators/reconstruction.jl `_ppm_limit_cw84_sym` (the symbolic-tracing-safe form using ifelse) bit-for-bit at the AST level. Identical block to discretizations/finite_volume/vertical_remap.json `limiter`.
limiter.name: colella_woodward_1984

is_extremum.comment: True when q[i] is a local extremum within the stencil — flatten the parabola to a constant.

overshoot_left.comment: Right edge sits past the parabola's interior extremum; pull the LEFT edge inward.

overshoot_right.comment: Left edge sits past the parabola's interior extremum; pull the RIGHT edge inward.

flux_integral.comment: CW84 §4 Courant-fraction flux integral. Within the upwind cell, integrate the (limited) parabolic profile over the swept fraction `|c|` of the cell; the result is the area-averaged value crossing the interface. Closed-form expressions used in both branches: `dq = qr - ql`, `q6 = 6·(qi − (ql + qr)/2)`, `c_abs = |c|`. The full per-interface flux is `v · ifelse(c >= 0, int_left, int_right)` with the upwind branch encoded directly in the AST — no caller-side branching on sign(c).

int_left.comment: Positive flow (c >= 0): upwind cell is the LEFT cell. Reconstruction (ql_L, qr_L, qm1) is CW84-limited above. Integrate from x = 1 - c_abs to x = 1.

int_right.comment: Negative flow (c < 0): upwind cell is the RIGHT cell. Reconstruction (ql_R, qr_R, q0) is CW84-limited above. Integrate from x = 0 to x = c_abs.

flux_form.comment: Final face-flux composition: PPM-reconstructed-and-limited cell-edge values from each side of the interface, integrated over the Courant-swept fraction of the upwind cell, multiplied by the face velocity. Upwind cell selection is encoded by `ifelse(c >= 0, int_left, int_right)`; no caller-side branching on sign(c). The CFL-violation guard `|c| <= 1` of the reference imperative implementation (`ppm_flux_integral`) is enforced by the caller via `compute_courant_numbers` clamping — the AST integrand is mathematically continuous beyond `|c| = 1` but no longer represents a swept-volume average.

flux_1d_ppm.composition_hint: Caller computes the face-staggered Courant `c_{i+1/2} = u_{i+1/2}·dt/dx` (uniform Cartesian) or via the cubed-sphere panel-boundary-aware accessor (`_get_courant_xi/_get_courant_eta` in src/operators/flux_1d.jl) and the face velocity `v_{i+1/2}`; this rule then yields the per-face PPM flux. The cell-centred tendency follows from the FV divergence `T_i = -(F_{i+1/2}·dx_{i+1/2} - F_{i-1/2}·dx_{i-1/2}) / A_i` (or composes with `divergence_arakawa_c` on a C-grid). Sibling rule to `lax_friedrichs_flux` — same `op=flux` shape, same per-face `$c`/`$v` binding contract, but high-order PPM upwind in place of first-order LF.

schema_gaps.comment: Three ESS schema extensions are required before this rule is fully materializable from JSON alone. Until they land, the runtime path is src/operators/flux_1d.jl (`flux_1d_ppm!` for the loop form; `flux_1d_ppm_arrayop` for the symbolic form). The rule file is authored declaratively (matching the `vertical_remap` precedent for nonlinear PPM rules and `weno5_advection`'s closed-AST nonlinear-weights pattern) so the math, references, and AST fragments are reproducible.

face_staggered_courant_binding.tracker: shared with discretizations/finite_volume/lax_friedrichs_flux.json (dsc-35x follow-ups); same gap as the latlon `cos_lat` per dsc-7jc, scoped here as the PPM-specific instance of dsc-cuj's stagger-aware binding follow-up.
face_staggered_courant_binding.summary: The face-Courant `$c` and face-velocity `$v` are per-face bindings (analogous to the slope-ratio `$r` in flux_limiter_minmod), not stencil-offset selectors. ESS's verify_mms_convergence today binds only scalar per-axis spacing symbols at sweep time; until per-face bindings land, walker fixtures must declare applicable=false.

ghost_extended_input_contract.tracker: discretizations/SELECTOR_KINDS.md (face-stagger / time-varying bindings follow-ups off dsc-35x).
ghost_extended_input_contract.summary: The 6-point cartesian stencil consumes cell-averaged values at offsets {-3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2} relative to the right cell of the interface. At domain (or panel) boundaries the {-3, -2} and {+2} offsets reach outside the physical domain; the imperative path resolves this by passing a ghost-extended q_ext (Ng = 3 ghost cells per side). ESS's stencil contract today does not declare how a rule consumes ghost cells — pending a `boundary_policy` / `ghost_width` rule-level field this contract has to live in prose.

panel_boundary_distance_handling.summary: On the cubed-sphere wrapper (`flux_1d_ppm!` / `flux_1d_ppm_arrayop`) the per-face Courant uses `grid.dist_xi_bnd` / `grid.dist_eta_bnd` at panel-boundary interfaces and `grid.dist_xi` / `grid.dist_eta` interior. The Cartesian core encoded here uses a single `$c` per face and ignores the panel split; the cubed-sphere wrapper builds a single distance array via `_build_interface_distances` and then applies this rule per face. Encoding the panel split in-rule needs the `boundary_policy` schema feature.

limitations: Cubed-sphere wrapper (src/operators/flux_1d.jl): in-panel coefficients match the Cartesian core verbatim; cross-panel ghost extension and panel-boundary distance handling live in the cubed_sphere accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl) and in `_build_interface_distances`. Same convention as `lax_friedrichs_flux` and the limiter rules — the rule references in-panel offsets only and is not yet expressible across the panel split because the cubed_sphere selector kind has no face-stagger slot.

Cross-panel boundary-flux matching (`_match_boundary_fluxes_xi!`, `_match_boundary_fluxes_eta!`, `_match_rotated_boundary_fluxes!` in src/operators/flux_1d.jl) is a post-stencil mass-flux average over the two panels' independently computed values. This is a non-linear post-processing step (parallel to the FV3 eq. 6.6 monotonicity clamp documented as a non-linear post-processing step in `ppm_edge_cubed_sphere`) and lives outside the §7 stencil contract.
